questions.question: 1. Compression testing is primarily used to study the behaviour of a material under ________.

explanations.a: Incorrect. Tensile loading is studied using a tensile test.
explanations.b: Correct. A compression test evaluates the behaviour of a material subjected to compressive loading.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Impact loading is studied using impact testing.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Torsional loading is studied using torsion testing.

questions.question: 2. Compression tests are commonly performed on brittle materials such as concrete and cast iron.

explanations.a: Correct. Brittle materials are generally strong in compression and are frequently evaluated using compression tests.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Compression testing is commonly used to evaluate brittle materials.

questions.question: 3. Which of the following is a common engineering member subjected primarily to compression?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Tie rods primarily carry tensile loads.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Cables are designed to resist tension.
explanations.c: Correct. Columns are structural members that primarily resist compressive loads.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Chains are intended to carry tensile forces.

questions.question: 4. Which specimen shape is commonly used in compression testing?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Cylindrical specimens are also commonly used.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Cubical specimens are also widely used.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Prismatic specimens are not the most common standard shapes.
explanations.d: Correct. Both cube and cylindrical specimens are commonly used in compression testing.

explanations.a: Incorrect. Multiplying load and area does not give stress.
explanations.b: Correct. Compressive stress is the applied load divided by the original cross-sectional area.
explanations.c: Incorrect. This does not represent stress.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Length is not part of the stress formula.

questions.question: 6. During a compression test, the specimen generally ________.

explanations.a: Incorrect. Elongation occurs during tensile loading.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Twisting is associated with torsion.
explanations.c: Correct. Compression causes the specimen to shorten in the direction of loading.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Melting is unrelated to compression testing.

questions.question: 7. During compression testing of cast iron, failure typically occurs along a:

explanations.a: Incorrect. This is not the typical failure pattern in cast iron compression tests.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Brittle compression failure usually does not occur on a horizontal plane.
explanations.c: Correct. Cast iron commonly develops cracks along an inclined or diagonal plane under compression.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Circular failure planes are not characteristic of cast iron compression failure.

questions.question: 8. The compressive strength of a material is:

answers.b: The average compressive stress during testing
answers.c: The stress at the proportional limit
answers.d: The maximum compressive stress sustained before failure

explanations.a: Incorrect. Compressive strength is not based on the minimum stress.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Average stress does not define compressive strength.
explanations.c: Incorrect. The proportional limit occurs before failure.
explanations.d: Correct. Compressive strength is the maximum compressive stress a material can withstand before failure.

questions.question: 9. Which material generally exhibits large plastic deformation before failure in compression?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Cast iron is relatively brittle.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Concrete is a brittle material.
explanations.c: Correct. Mild steel is ductile and undergoes significant plastic deformation under compression.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Brick behaves as a brittle material.
